title: tk graph: do not print the close-it hint for already-closed epics
description:
Cleanup carried forward from the phase 2 retro (hbz epic notes, known nit): tk graph on an epic whose children are all closed prints "Epic <id> is complete — all child ticks are closed. Close it with tk close <id>." even when the EPIC ITSELF is already closed — observed live: tk graph azw suggests closing the already-closed azw.

Fix in cmd/tk/cmd/graph.go (handleChildlessEpic and/or its caller): when the epic status is closed, print a completed-epic message without the close-it instruction, e.g. "Epic <id> is closed (all <n> child ticks closed)." JSON: needs_planning stays false; no new fields.

Add a test alongside the existing all_closed_children tests covering the closed-epic variant.

Run: go test ./cmd/... && go vet ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./cmd/... passes; tk graph on a closed epic no longer suggests closing it (test-covered)
